github.comment: Optional. Create a repo on GitHub, put its URL here and set enable_push=true. See SETUP.md.

public_sync.comment: After each successful private push, mirror ONLY the publishable subset (harness code + dashboard shell, never the AI/DS/ML/DL paper trees) to the PUBLIC repo agentic-research-advisor. Allowlist-first with a hard leak guard (scripts/public_sync.py). Active by default; set enabled=false to stop mirroring.

email.comment: Optional. To receive daily reports, set enabled=true and provide a Gmail App Password OR a SendGrid API key. See SETUP.md.
email.comment_only_new: When true, each run only emails papers not delivered before (tracked in state/emailed_ledger.json by paper slug). Run send_report.py --all to bypass and resend the whole corpus.
email.comment_quiet_hours: Emails transmit only in [send_from, hold_start) local time; anything triggered outside is held (state/email_pending.json) and released at send_from by the session-runner. send_from doubles as the release time. --ignore-quiet-hours overrides; enabled=false disables.

harvest.comment_areas: Free-text queries drive the keyword sources (Semantic Scholar/OpenAlex/Crossref). arXiv is driven by arxiv_categories instead: it sorts by submittedDate not relevance, so a bare term like 'Data Science' floods the corpus with off-topic physics/astro. Categories keep each area on-topic and disjoint.

areas.AI: artificial intelligence agents reasoning planning large language model alignment
areas.DS: data science statistical analysis tabular data feature engineering causal inference information retrieval
areas.ML: machine learning supervised learning generalization optimization model training benchmark
areas.DL: deep learning neural network transformer architecture representation learning computer vision

harvest.comment: Relative paths are resolved against the repo root.
harvest.comment_wiki_relevance: Bias harvest selection toward papers relevant to the LLM knowledge wiki: added to rank_score as weight*relevance (0..1 TF-IDF cosine to the closest concept pages). Availability (downloadable PDF, 1000) still dominates, recency is 500; 400 makes relevance a strong tie-breaker. Set 0 to disable.

reproduce.comment: claude_exe: leave empty to use the CLAUDE_EXE env var or the first `claude` found on PATH.
reproduce.comment_wiki_context_pages: Top-K most relevant LLM-wiki concept pages injected as grounding context into each paper's reproduction prompt (0 disables).
reproduce.comment_max_retries: A retryable infra failure (usage/session limit, API 529, empty/timeout-no-output) re-attempts a paper up to this many times before it settles as EXHAUSTED_RETRIES; terminal outcomes and real reproductions are never retried.
reproduce.comment_pacing: Quota pacing so a cycle works WITH the account budget instead of bursting into the quota wall (see reproduce.py header). consecutive_limit_stop: stop the cycle after this many CONSECUTIVE papers hit a usage/session/API limit, 0 disables; un-attempted papers retry next cycle (outcome-based dedup does not burn them). min_seconds_between_calls: minimum wall-clock gap enforced between headless-claude spawns, 0 disables.
reproduce.comment_per_paper_minutes: Raised 35->50 on 2026-07-17: 4 papers died at exactly the 35-min cap with substantial src/ written (TIMEOUT). Combined with continuation retries this gives a segmented budget of up to max_retries x 50 min per paper.

paths.comment: All optional. python: empty = the interpreter running the scripts. repo_root: empty = the repo containing scripts/. wiki_project: empty = <repo_root>/AI_DS_ML_DL.
